ch01.title: The Evolution Path
ch01.intro: In 26 years, emoji grew from one mobile carrier's 176 12×12-pixel icons into a 3,790+ cross-platform, cross-cultural visual vocabulary.

docomo-1999.narrative: Shigetaka Kurita designed 176 12×12-pixel icons for NTT DoCoMo's i-mode service.

iphone-jp-2007.narrative: SoftBank brought an emoji keyboard to Japanese iPhone users, accelerating emoji's spread across global mobile platforms.

emoji-6-0.narrative: 722 emojis enter the Unicode Standard, unifying the emoji sets from Japan's three major carriers.

emoji-7-0.narrative: Unicode 7.0 adds roughly 250 symbols — including Webdings and Wingdings characters — dramatically expanding the emoji vocabulary.

emoji-8-0.narrative: 5 Fitzpatrick skin-tone modifiers introduced; diverse family combinations gain visual representation.

emoji-11-0.narrative: 157 new emojis arrive including superheroes and hot/cold faces; the version number jumps from 5.0 to 11.0 to align with the Unicode Standard.

emoji-12-0.narrative: 230 new emojis (including variants) added, bringing gender-neutral figures and accessibility-themed characters into the standard.

emoji-12-1.narrative: 168 emojis updated, further expanding gender-neutral person sequences for greater inclusivity in human representation.

emoji-13-0.narrative: 117 new emojis debut, with 🥲 the smiling-face-with-tear capturing the bittersweet emotional register of the pandemic year.

emoji-13-1.narrative: 217 new sequences released, including heart-hand modifiers and cross-skin-tone handshakes, enriching human-connection expressions.

emoji-14-0.narrative: 112 new emojis bring heart-hands 🫶, melting face 🫠, and saluting face 🫡, deepening the gesture and emotion vocabulary.

emoji-15-0.narrative: 31 new emojis including pink heart 🩷 and shaking face 🫨 reflect the ongoing refinement of nuanced emotional expression.

emoji-15-1.narrative: 118 new emojis including direction-flipped face sequences demonstrate Unicode's support for multidirectional human-body motion.

emoji-16-0.narrative: Only 8 new emojis added, signaling a shift from expansion to refinement in the standardization cycle.

apple-genmoji.narrative: Apple's iOS 18 introduces Genmoji: natural-language-prompted personalized emoji.

ch02.title: Who Gets In
ch02.intro: About 100 proposals are submitted to Unicode each year. The acceptance rate is roughly 30–60% — and it hinges on more than technical merit.

dumpling.story: Designer Yiying Lu helped push for Asian-food cultural representation; the dumpling shipped with Unicode 10.0 in 2017.

ch03.title: Always On
ch03.intro: Gen Z is the first generation that has never put the phone out of sight. For them, emoji aren't decoration — they're a new grammar, punctuation, and tone of voice.

semantic.heading: Same emoji, different meaning by generation
semantic.disclosure: Based on cited reporting and journalism — not a primary survey dataset.

ch04.title: Beyond the Screen
ch04.intro: 26 years on, emoji are escaping the keyboard — generated, dimensionalized, worn on faces, made into brands, and stepped into space.

apple-genmoji.title: Genmoji
apple-genmoji.story: iOS 18 generates personalized emoji from a natural-language prompt. Every user can now produce their own.

footer.heading: Sources & Methodology
footer.credits: Every quantitative claim on this page carries a public, citable source.
